data.title: UK electricals retailer Currys raises profit outlook after robust trading
data.description:
British electricals retailer Currys on Thursday raised its full-year profit forecast, saying trading since early January had been robust.

The group, which sells consumer electricals from computers to washing machines and TVs, said adjusted profit before tax in its 2024/25 year was now expected to be around 160 million pounds ($209 million), up from previous guidance of 145 million-155 million pounds.

($1 = 0.7638 pounds)

data.title: South Korea May Seek Tariff Reduction by Offering to Buy More U.S. GoodsMarket Talk
data.description: South Korea could seek a reduction in U.S. tariffs by offering to purchase more U.S. natural gas, oil, weaponry and commercial airplanes, as well as by increasing investing in the U.S., Citigroup analysts Jin-Wook Kim and Jiuk Choi say in a note. Seoul could also lower non-trade barriers and take on a greater share of the costs for stationing U.S. troops in South Korea to mitigate tariff risks, they say. On Wednesday, President Trump announced a strict 25% reciprocal tariff on South Korea, in addition to a separate 25% tariffs on autos. Citi expects the Bank of Korea to cut its policy rate again in May to support the economy but doesn't rule out an earlier-than-expected cut in April, citing the "sharper-than-expected" impact of U.S. reciprocal tariffs. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Ireland 10Y Bond Yield Hits 14-month Low
data.description:
Ireland 10 Year Government Bond Yield decreased to a 14-month low of 2.33%.

Over the past 4 weeks, Ireland 10Y Bond Yield lost 44.20 basis points, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 48.10 basis points.

data.title: Wise targets double-digit pretax profit margin by FY 2026
data.description:
Wise plc expects to deliver underlying income growth of 15-20% and underlying pretax profit margin at the upper end of its target range in its 2026 financial year, citing strong progress on its "money without borders" vision.

The British money transfer firm, which is hosting a London-based capital markets event on Thursday, said it was targeting underlying pretax profit margin of 13-16% over the medium term and an underlying income compound annual growth rate of 15-20%.

"Wise customers have moved more than half a trillion across borders and trust us to now hold more than 20 billion pounds of their money," the company said in a statement.

Thursday's presentation will include preliminary figures for its 2025 financial year that will show 21% growth year-on-year in active customers to 15.5 million, a 22% rise in cross border volumes to 145 billion pounds and a 16% rise in underlying income to 1.36 billion pounds.

Wise said its board had consulted extensively with shareholders on optimal listing arrangements for company and expects to share the outcome of a review of its listing arrangements with investors in the coming weeks.

data.title: Moonpig Group Forecasts Growth in FY25 Revenue, Adjusted EPS
data.description:
Moonpig Group said Thursday that it expects revenue and adjusted EPS for fiscal 2025 to grow year over year.

Revenue for the 12 months ending April 30 is expected to be between 350 million pounds sterling and 353 million pounds, compared with 341.1 million pounds in the prior fiscal year.

Meanwhile, the online greeting card and gifting platform's adjusted EPS for fiscal 2025 is expected to grow by a double-digit percentage.

For further information:

Picton

Kathy Thompson, Company Secretary

020 7011 9988, kathy.thompson@picton.co.uk

About Picton

Established in 2005, Picton is listed on the main market of the London Stock Exchange and is a constituent of a number of EPRA indices including the FTSE EPRA Nareit Global Index.

Picton owns and actively manages a £737 million UK commercial property portfolio, invested across 48 assets and with around 350 occupiers (as at 31 December 2024).

Through an occupier focused, opportunity led approach, Picton aims to be one of the consistently best performing diversified UK REITs and has delivered upper quartile outperformance and a consistently higher income return than the MSCI Quarterly Property Index since launch. 

With a portfolio strategically positioned to capture income and capital growth, currently weighted towards the industrial sector, Picton's agile business model provides flexibility to adapt to evolving market trends over the long-term.

Picton has a responsible approach to business and is committed to being net zero carbon by 2040.

For more information please visit:www.picton.co.uk

data.title: King International Shares Jump 14% on Withdrawal of Winding-up Petition
data.description:
King International Investment received a court notice from the Hong Kong High Court confirming the withdrawal of a winding-up petition against the company.

This withdrawal was based on the joint application of both parties' solicitors, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The petition, filed by Mianyang Zhongshang Fule Wine in January, sought the recovery of 56.4 million yuan in alleged overdue payment under a sales of goods agreement dated April 9, 2024.

The liquor seller said it would conduct an audit and do due diligence on the disputed transaction.

Shares of King International surged almost 14% in afternoon trade on Thursday.

data.title: Dollar Hits Near 6-Month Low on Trump’s Tariffs
data.description:
The dollar index fell below 103 on Thursday, hitting its lowest level in nearly six months as traders digested President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariff measures.

Late Wednesday, Trump announced a comprehensive tariff package aimed at reshaping US trade relationships.

The measures include a 10% baseline tariff on all imports, with higher rates for key trade partners, such as China (34%), the European Union (20%), and Japan (24%).

Additionally, a 25% tariff on all foreign-made automobiles took immediate effect.

Meanwhile, fresh economic data painted a mixed picture.

The ADP report showed a stronger-than-expected 155K increase in private-sector jobs for March, surpassing forecasts.

However, the JOLTS report revealed that job openings fell to 7.57 million, signaling a potential slowdown in the labor market.

Investors are now turning their attention to Friday’s nonfarm payrolls report, which could provide further insight into the Federal Reserve’s next policy move.

data.title: Abu Dhabi's Taqa, Ewec to Develop 1GW Open-Cycle Gas Turbine Project
data.description:
Abu Dhabi National Energy (ADX:TAQA), d/b/a Taqa, said Thursday it is collaborating with water and electricity supplier Emirates Water and Electricity, d/b/a Ewec, to develop and implement new energy infrastructure projects in the United Arab Emirates.

The utility company signed a 24-year power purchase agreement with Ewec to build, own, and operate the Al Dhafra open-cycle gas turbine project. Taqa will lead the project and undertake the operation and maintenance of the plant.

Additionally, Taqa unit Taqa Transmission will develop a power grid infrastructure to integrate the new capacity to new energy demand sources.

data.title: Russia Services PMI Falls to 9-Month Low
data.description:
The S&P Global Russia Services PMI declined to 50.1 in March 2025, down from 50.5 in February, marking the softest expansion in the sector since last June, when the index posted a contraction.

The latest reading also marked the ninth consecutive month of growth in the services sector, but nearly stagnation, amid muted demand conditions.

New business growth eased for the second straight month, due to weakening purchasing power.

Employment fell for the first time since July 2023 and the sharpest in just over two years, while backlogs of work rose for the fifth month running.

On prices, input cost inflation eased to the second-lowest since July 2020, while output inflation slowed to the softest since January 2021, amid efforts to stimulate new sales.

Lastly, business sentiment improved to a 10-month high due to hopes of stronger demand conditions.

data.title: Mdundo.com Hires Financial Adviser for Strategic Review
data.description:
Mdundo.com appointed Deloitte Corporate Finance South Africa as a financial adviser for its strategic review, according to a Thursday release.

The South African telecommunications company noted that the review might result in a merger, consolidation, or ownership change, among others. It cautioned that there is no certainty that a deal might materialize.

data.title: Parag Milk Gets Board Nod to Raise INR1.6 Billion via Preferential Issue of Convertible Warrants; Shares Up 7%
data.description:
Parag Milk Foods has received board approval to raise 1.61 billion Indian rupees through the preferential issue of convertible warrants, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company will float a preferential issue of 9 million convertible warrants to the proposed allottees convertible into 9 million equity shares of face value of 10 rupees each fully paid up for 179.10 rupees.

Shares of the company were up nearly 7% in recent trade.

data.title: Italian Stocks Decline on Trump’s Expansive Tariffs
data.description:
The FTSE MIB Index futures tumbled 1.7% to below 38,000 on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump’s unveiled expansive global tariffs.

Trump imposed a 10% baseline tariff on goods from all countries and higher duties on some of the country's biggest trading partners, including a 20% levy on imports from the EU. Also, Trump’s 25% tariffs on imported vehicles have taken effect.

European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the EU was prepared to respond with countermeasures if talks with the U.S. failed.

On Wednesday, Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and its top business lobby warned that U.S. tariffs would have a major impact on the country's exports and already struggling economy.

On the economic front, traders will assess Italy’s PMI data for insights into the health of the country’s economy.

data.title: CAC 40 Futures Fall Amid Trump’s Sweeping Tariffs
data.description:
The CAC 40 futures dropped 0.3% to 7,691 on Thursday, marking its lowest level in over two months as investor sentiment soured following US President Donald Trump’s aggressive and far-reaching tariff measures.

Trump announced reciprocal tariffs on over 180 countries and territories, including a 20% levy on European Union goods, an immediate 25% tariff on imported cars, and a 10% baseline tariff on imports from all nations.

These actions further intensified the global trade war, fueling concerns over rising inflation and the potential slowdown in both US and global economic growth.

European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen described the tariffs as a significant blow to the global economy, while also warning that the EU is preparing countermeasures if negotiations fail.

Meanwhile, investors are closely awaiting PMI reports from across Europe, looking for insights into the region’s economic outlook.

data.title: Currys Lifts Profit Guidance as Sales Improve
data.description:
(06:00 GMT) *Currys PLC Profits Expected to Be Above Previous Guidance
(06:01 GMT) *Currys PLC Sees Trading Above Views
(06:01 GMT) *Currys PLC Trading Since Jan 4 Has Been Robust
(06:02 GMT) *Currys PLC Experienced Continued Positive LFL Sales Growth in UK&I, Nordics
(06:02 GMT) *Currys PLC Raises FY View
(06:02 GMT) *Currys Sees FY Adjusted Pretax Profit of GBP160M
(06:03 GMT) *Currys PLC Had Seen FY Adjusted Pretax Profit of GBP145M-GBP155M
(06:03 GMT) *Currys PLC Expects to Finish Yr in a Strong Net Cash Position
(06:14 GMT) Currys Lifts Profit Guidance as Sales Improve

By Michael Susin

Currys said it expects profit for the fiscal year to be ahead of its previous guidance, supported by continuing sales growth in its key markets.

The retailer of technology products said Thursday that it now expects adjusted pretax profit--the company's preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--for the year ending May 3 to be around 160 million pounds ($208.1 million) compared with its previous guidance range of between 145 million and 155 million pounds and fiscal 2024's 118 million pounds.

The company said its performance since January has been strong, with continued like-for-like sales growth in both the U.K. and Ireland and the Nordics markets.

Currys expects to finish the year in a strong net cash position.

The company is scheduled to report its fiscal 2025 update on May 21.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com
